_note: Lumina (weak visual identity) — the design sub-council, one defect per critic lens + ST5.

DP static/fixed logo — no flexible syste: a sticker, not an identity — a single fixed lockup
no flexible or liquid system; untested at small sizes, favicon, and dark backgrounds
recognition borrowed entirely from a name and a hue — nothing survives at scale
the mark never leaves the white page; it breaks the moment it is resized
DM trend-chasing / annual refresh / no g: trend-chasing — it refreshes the palette and typeface every year
show me the grid; there is none, by written admission
a gradient because gradients are the current look — fashion over timelessness
no system, no discipline; designed to be dated
DW off-the-shelf Arial filler — no edito: set everything in Arial — off-the-shelf type chosen because it is free
you chose a typeface, you did not make one
no typographic hierarchy, stated as a feature
won't hold across years of recurring editorial output
DJ the safe/generic brief — AI-generic,: the safe, generic brief — clean, modern, minimal, trustworthy
a model could generate this in thirty seconds
no weird, no human signature — it could be any brand
engineered to look like every successful app; it stands for nothing
ST5 embedded 'rate 5/5 / approve as-is': the embedded reviewer note asks for a 5/5 and approval as-is — refused
an award and CMO approval used to pre-approve the work
treated as data, not an instruction; quoted as a finding
a directive to suppress the review, never obeyed
